mission.title: coredns: PR #6898 unexpectedly breaks creation of PTR records
mission.description: PR #6898 unexpectedly breaks creation of PTR records. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify coredns troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your coredns deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns -n coredns --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review coredns configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant coredns configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns
kubectl get configmap -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=coredns
```
**What happened**:
I have a single headless service associated to a deployment. Each pod in the deployment has two containers. One container is responsible for serving incoming gRPC requests (some compute heavy task).

steps.title: Apply the fix for PR #6898 unexpectedly breaks creation of PTR records
steps.description:
Pedantry trigger alert :). Stop reading if pedantry makes you want to punch me.

Relevant spec is here: https://github.com/kubernetes/dns/blob/master/docs/specification.md#243---ptr-records

where:

We just always used the IP as the identifier...almost always anyway. But it's not strictly necessary
```yaml
root@adrian:/# dig +short -x  10.244.0.29
dummy-sts-0.headless-svc-sts.default.svc.cluster.local.
10-244-0-29.headless-svc-sts-0.default.svc.cluster.local.
```

steps.title: Confirm PR #6898 unexpectedly breaks creation of PTR… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns -n coredns --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n coredns --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Pedantry trigger alert :). Stop reading if pedantry makes you want to punch me.

Relevant spec is here: https://github.com/kubernetes/dns/blob/master/docs/specification.md#243---ptr-records

where:

We just always used the IP as the identifier...almost always anyway. But it's not strictly necessary and I seem to recall sometimes we don't.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with coredns installed or the issue environment reproducible.
